protocol.name: sprint-status-write-safety
protocol.description: Behavioral contract for writing to sprint-status.yaml. This file is written by multiple workflows and must be treated as a shared resource. Story file frontmatter is the source of truth; sprint-status.yaml is a derived view.

rule.title: Story File Is Source of Truth

rules.rule: The story file frontmatter Status field is authoritative for story status. sprint-status.yaml is a derived/cached view. When they diverge, the story file wins. The /kratos-sprint-status sync reconciles drift.

rule.title: Review Workflows Do Not Write sprint-status.yaml

rules.rule: The 6 review workflows (code-review, qa-generate-tests, security-review, test-automation, test-review, performance-review) MUST NOT write to sprint-status.yaml. They update only the story file (Review Gate table and frontmatter status). The sprint-status sync reconciles later.
Any workflow that writes to sprint-status.yaml MUST re-read the file immediately before writing. Never rely on a cached or earlier read. The re-read must happen in the same step as the write.
When updating sprint-status.yaml, modify ONLY the story entry you are responsible for. Do not rewrite entries for other stories. Preserve all fields you are not explicitly changing.
Running /kratos-sprint-status reconciles sprint-status.yaml with story file frontmatter. If data drifts, the sync corrects it. This is the recovery mechanism for any write anomalies.
